2606.27909.title: Triadic Werewolf: A Jester Role for Multi-Hop Theory of Mind in LLMs
2606.27909.paper_summary: Theory-of-mind evaluations of large language models typically use dyadic social-deduction games, where every observable cue points to a single hidden side, so a model with strong language priors can score well without ever simulating opponents' incentives. We extend the Werewolf game with a Jester, a third faction whose utility on peer suspicion is inverted because it wins by being voted out, so optimal play requires reasoning across three opposing utility functions. Across 60 games on GPT-4.1, DeepSeek-V3.1, and Llama-3.3-70B with Jester self-learning on and off, the Jester wins 60-70% of games while Werewolves never exceed 20%, and GPT-4.1 wolves vote the Jester out on day 1 in 60-70% of games, a strictly self-defeating action. Self-learning helps DeepSeek and Llama but hurts GPT-4.1, with the cost landing on Villagers rather than Werewolves. Only DeepSeek learns the subtle strategy of looking suspicious without looking intentionally suspicious, and it gains the most from the loop. Triadic incentive structure exposes a layer of multi-agent reasoning that dyadic deduction games leave invisible.
2606.27909.paper_summary_zh: 心智理論對大型語言模型的評估通常使用二人社交推理遊戲，其中每個可觀察的線索都指向一個隱藏的面向，因此擁有強大語言先驗的模型可以在不模擬對手動機的情況下表現良好。我們擴展了狼人遊戲，引入了一個小丑，這是一個第三方勢力，其在同儕懷疑上的效用是反向的，因為它通過被投票淘汰來獲勝，因此最佳玩法需要在三個對立的效用函數之間進行推理。在60場關於GPT-4.1、DeepSeek-V3.1和Llama-3.3-70B的遊戲中，無論小丑自我學習開啟或關閉，小丑贏得了60-70%的比賽，而狼人從未超過20%，而GPT-4.1的狼人在60-70%的遊戲中在第一天就將小丑投票淘汰，這是一個嚴格的自我挫敗行為。自我學習對DeepSeek和Llama有幫助，但對GPT-4.1卻有害，成本落在村民身上而不是狼人身上。只有DeepSeek學會了在不故意看起來可疑的情況下看起來可疑的微妙策略，並且它從這個循環中獲得了最多的收益。三元激勵結構揭示了一層多代理推理，而二元推理遊戲則將其隱藏。
2606.27909.author: Avni Mittal et.al.
